Despite this, the company updated its 2026 guidance to a more conservative revenue range of $109 million to $114 million and adjusted EBITDA of $21 million to $25 million due to a market shift away from managed services and client conservatism driven by MFN pricing. OptimizeRx leadership expressed confidence in long-term value creation, highlighted by a $10 million share repurchase program and the view that AI will be a tailwind, not a disruption, for their business model.

feed.title: Full House Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ:FLL) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Full House Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ:FLL) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, missing EPS expectations with a negative $0.34 EPS against an estimated $-0.23477. The company discussed revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth, particularly at its American Place temporary casino and improvements at Chamonix following management changes. Full House Resorts also provided updates on the financing and construction timeline for the permanent American Place casino and the status of its smaller properties and sports wagering business.

feed.title: Ero Copper Corp. (NYSE:ERO) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Ero Copper Corp. (NYSE: ERO) discussed its Q4 2025 operating and financial results, highlighting record mill throughput at Caraiba, increased copper production at Tucuma, and significant gold concentrate sales from Xavantina. The company also unveiled a preliminary economic analysis for its Furnas project, projecting substantial copper, gold, and silver production over 24 years with a strong after-tax NPV and IRR. Ero Copper aims to continue deleveraging its balance sheet and anticipates potentially returning capital to shareholders once its net debt-to-EBITDA ratio falls below 1x.

feed.title: Life360 Stock Set for Breakout Amid Historic Profitability: Why an $89 Target Is Next
feed.summary: Life360 (LIF) achieved its first fully profitable year in company history in 2025, with Q4 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA significantly exceeding analyst estimates. The company is transitioning into a full-stack advertising platform following the acquisition of Nativo and is also expanding its "Pet GPS" product, aiming for a Street target price of $89.97, representing a 104.6% potential return. This growth is underpinned by its proprietary first-party data, aggressive expansion, and an AI and advertising advantage.

feed.title: Booking Holdings’ AI Push Tests Role In Trip Planning And Payments
feed.summary: Booking Holdings is integrating advanced AI tools into its brands like Booking.com and Priceline to adapt to evolving travel search behaviors. The company aims to leverage its "merchant of record" role to maintain its central position in trip planning and payments, even as large AI platforms influence initial discovery. This strategic move, however, introduces execution risks regarding conversion and potential margin compression, alongside competitive pressures from other online travel agencies.

feed.title: AbbVie Stock Analysis: Performance, Financials, and Outlook - News and Statistics
feed.summary: AbbVie's stock has outperformed the S&P 500 over the last six months, driven by strong quarterly results and solid financial performance, including high free cash flow margins. feed.title: Guardant Health’s Shield Tour Links Rural Access And Growth Expectations
feed.summary: Guardant Health has launched its Shield Across America mobile tour to bring its FDA-approved Shield blood test for colorectal cancer screening to over 100 underserved rural communities. This initiative, coinciding with National Colorectal Cancer Awareness Month, highlights the company's effort to increase adoption of its convenient blood-based screening option. While the tour addresses a critical public health need and could boost test utilization, investors should consider the company's unprofitability and increased spending against the potential for revenue growth and expanded market share.

feed.title: Wabtec Settlement With Progress Rail Clears Path For Rail Growth
feed.summary: Wabtec (NYSE:WAB) and Progress Rail have settled their legal dispute, removing uncertainty for Wabtec's rail business and allowing both companies to focus on core rail industry themes. This resolution is seen as beneficial for investors, enabling Wabtec to prioritize contract execution, aftermarket support, and digital solutions, aligning with the narrative of rail modernization and growth. However, investors should also consider the risks of lagging organic revenue growth and reliance on acquisitions amidst this positive development.

feed.title: Does Onshape’s New Cloud MBD Capability Deepen PTC’s (PTC) Role in End‑to‑End Product Lifecycles?
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has launched a cloud-native Model-Based Definition (MBD) capability within its Onshape CAD and PDM platform, allowing engineering teams to embed manufacturing information directly into 3D models. This move aims to centralize product and manufacturing data, enhancing PTC's role in customers' end-to-end product lifecycles and AI-driven process improvements. While it strengthens PTC's intelligent product lifecycle story and growth catalysts, the article notes that investors should also consider ongoing risks from competition and market uncertainty.

feed.title: Is West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) Pricing Look Justified After Mixed Multi Year Share Performance
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) stock has shown mixed performance over recent years, with gains over 30 days and 1 year, but declines over 3 and 5 years. feed.title: Uber Air Taxis In Dubai Test Multi Modal Platform Ambitions
feed.summary: Uber Technologies is partnering with Joby Aviation to launch an all-electric air taxi service in Dubai, integrated directly into the Uber app for combined air and ground transport bookings. This move represents Uber's ambition to expand beyond traditional ride-hailing into multi-modal transportation and leverage future mobility trends. While it's a test of Uber's platform for higher-priced, premium travel without the risk of aircraft ownership, investors will be watching its success as a "proof of concept" rather than an immediate revenue driver.

feed.title: Does Onshape’s New Cloud MBD Capability Deepen PTC’s (PTC) Role in End‑to‑End Product Lifecycles?
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has introduced a new cloud-native Model-Based Definition (MBD) capability within its Onshape CAD and PDM platform. This feature allows engineering teams to embed manufacturing information directly into 3D models, aiming to centralize product and manufacturing data in a single cloud-native model. The initiative is intended to deepen PTC's role in customers' end-to-end product lifecycles and support AI-driven process improvements, although the article notes it doesn't immediately alter the focus on SaaS transition or mitigate competitive pressures.

feed.title: Ross opens 2 more Long Island stores as off-price chain pulls more budget-watching shoppers

Ross Stores Inc. plans to open 110 new stores this year, continuing its strategy to capitalize on consumers' desire for affordability amidst economic worries.

feed.title: Cummins' Fastest-Growing Business Isn't Trucks. It's Data Center Power.
feed.summary: Cummins (NYSE: CMI) is experiencing a significant surge in demand for its power generation equipment due to the artificial intelligence (AI) boom, with its power systems segment becoming its fastest-growing and most profitable business. The company's data center power solutions are critical for providing reliable standby energy, driving substantial revenue growth and margin expansion. Despite a cyclical slowdown in its traditional engine and components businesses, Cummins' overall financial performance is strong, and its stock is considered a reasonable play on the AI sector.

feed.title: AutoZone Q2 Earnings: New Store Openings Drive Growth But Weak Demand Weighs (NYSE:AZO)
feed.summary: AutoZone (AZO) reported Q2 EPS of $27.63, exceeding estimates, but revenue of $4.27 billion fell short, and same-store sales growth lagged expectations. While new store openings are driving overall growth, weak consumer demand and macroeconomic headwinds are impacting same-store sales and profitability, with both gross and operating margins contracting. The company is actively buying back shares and expanding internationally but faces long-term risks from increasing EV adoption and inventory management challenges.

feed.title: Crown Castle Weighs Fiber Exit To Refocus Towers And Support Dividends
feed.summary: Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) plans to divest its small cell and fiber businesses in the second half of 2026 to reduce leverage and improve EBITDA margins, aiming for a more concentrated U.S. tower model. This strategic shift is intended to simplify the business, potentially enhance profitability, and better support its dividends, despite risks related to execution and existing balance sheet pressures. Investors should monitor valuation updates, management commentary on future dividends, and tower performance compared to peers.

feed.title: TE Connectivity PLC (NYSE:TEL) Emerges as a Top-Tier Dividend Stock for Sustainable Income
